title: Add paid amount and balance amount fields to invoices

requirements.summary: Add Paid Amount input and auto-calculated Balance Due field to Create Invoice form
acceptanceCriteria: A numeric 'Paid Amount' input is shown in the totals section of the Create Invoice form.
A read-only 'Balance Due' field is shown, updating reactively as subtotal or paid amount changes.
The `paidAmount` value is submitted to the backend when creating the invoice.
Fields are styled consistently with the amber/teal design theme.

file_operations.description: Add paidAmount state (number, defaulting to 0) and a computed balanceDue value (subtotal - paidAmount). Add a Paid Amount numeric input field in the totals section below line items. Add a read-only Balance Due display field that updates reactively when subtotal or paidAmount changes. Pass paidAmount (or null if 0) to the createInvoice backend method when submitting the form. Style both fields consistently with the existing amber/teal theme and totals section design.

requirements.summary: Display Paid Amount and Balance Due on Invoice Detail page and in printable invoice layout
acceptanceCriteria: Invoice Detail page displays a 'Paid Amount' row in the totals section.
Invoice Detail page displays a 'Balance Due' row in the totals section.
The printable invoice layout (print/PDF output) includes both Paid Amount and Balance Due rows.
Fields are styled consistently with the amber/teal theme.
The 'Paid Amount' and 'Balance Due' rows are hidden from the printed layout only if the design requires it — otherwise they should appear in print as well.

file_operations.description: Add two new rows in the totals/summary section: one displaying 'Paid Amount' with the invoice.paidAmount value, and one displaying 'Balance Due' calculated as (invoice.total - invoice.paidAmount). Ensure both rows appear in the on-screen detail view and in the printable layout (within the print styles). Style consistently with the existing totals section and amber/teal theme. Handle graceful fallback for invoices where paidAmount may be undefined (default to 0).

requirements.summary: Update Invoice List and Dashboard to display balance/paid amounts and ensure stats remain accurate
acceptanceCriteria: Invoice List table shows 'Balance Due' or 'Paid Amount' as a column or within the row, clearly labeled.
Dashboard stats remain accurate and do not break with the new fields.
No undefined or NaN values appear for invoices created before the new fields were added (graceful fallback to 0).

file_operations.description: Add a 'Balance Due' column to the invoice table displaying (invoice.total - invoice.paidAmount) for each invoice. Ensure the column is clearly labeled and styled consistently with the existing table columns. Handle undefined paidAmount values gracefully by defaulting to 0 to avoid NaN or undefined display issues.

Review and update the revenue calculation logic to account for paidAmount if necessary, ensuring stats remain accurate. If the revenue stat currently sums invoice.total, consider whether it should instead sum invoice.paidAmount to reflect actual revenue received. Add graceful fallback logic to treat missing paidAmount as 0 to prevent NaN or undefined values in the summary stats. Optionally display total outstanding balance across all invoices in a new stat card.
